comment.shortText:
Vehicle handling, which affects how vehicle responds and reacts to the inputs of a driver.
This handling applies to all vehicle instances, as opposed to the {@link Vehicle.handling}.

tags.text: Changes will be reflected on every vehicle using this particular handling, but might require vehicle recreating for changes to apply properly.

comment.shortText: Initializes a new instance of the {@link HandlingData} class that can modify handling properties for the specified handling name.
Initializes a new instance of the {@link HandlingData} class that can modify handling properties for the specified handling name.

tags.text: See {@link getForHandlingName}.

comment.shortText: No particular usage for now, stick to {@link everyTick} instead.

Vehicle handling, which affects how vehicle responds and reacts to the inputs of a driver.
This handling applies to particular vehicle instance, as opposed to the {@link HandlingData}.

tags.text: Changes will be reflected only on a particular instance of the vehicle. On creation, model handling will be used as a base and changed properties will be added on top of it.

comment.shortText: Indicates whether vehicle handling was modified.
comment.returns: `true` if vehicle handling was modified; otherwise, `false`.

comment.shortText: Resets the vehicle handling back to a model handling.
Represents the current client branch.
Resource name of the executing entity.
Represents the current client SDK version.

tags.text: It's the version of the SDK the current runtime was compiled with.

comment.shortText: Represents the current client version.

tags.text: It's a slighty modified semantic versioning specification, which can be matched using this regular expression pattern `^(0|[1-9]\d*)\.(0|[1-9]\d*)(?:-((?:0|[1-9]\d*|\d*[a-zA-Z-][0-9a-zA-Z-]*)(?:\.(?:0|[1-9]\d*|\d*[a-zA-Z-][0-9a-zA-Z-]*))*))$`.

comment.shortText: Changes the visibility of cursor.

tags.text: This is handled by resource scoped internal integer, which gets increased/decreased by every function call. When you show your cursor 5 times, to hide it you have to do that 5 times accordingly.

comment.shortText: Object dimension.
Internal identificator of the entity which is identical on both sides.
Entity model hash.

tags.text: Only setter accepts string or number as input, getter returns value as number.

comment.shortText: Network owner of the entity.

tags.text:
Network owner is responsible for syncing entity with the server.
It changes when actual network owner passes the migration range,
then the new one is determined based on distance from the entity
(if entity is a vehicle, then the driver will take priority for becoming network owner).
Disabling migration range will stop this process from happening until turned on again.

tags.text: Values are provided in radians .

comment.shortText: Resets overwritten network owner.

tags.text:
See {@link Entity~netOwner} to understand how network owner works.

Keep in mind that disabling migration can lead to unexpected behaviour when the network owner gets out of the streaming range.

comment.text:
Pass true to disable migration, false to keep it enabled.
If not specified, it defaults to "false".

tags.text: This variable was renamed to {@link defaultDimension} on the other branches than release.
This variable was renamed to {@link globalDimension} on the other branches than release.

comment.shortText: Represents the current server branch.
Resource name of the executing entity.
Represents the current server SDK version.

tags.text: It's the version of the SDK the current runtime was compiled with.

comment.shortText: Represents the current server version.

tags.text: It's a slighty modified semantic versioning specification, which can be matched using this regular expression pattern `^(0|[1-9]\d*)\.(0|[1-9]\d*)(?:-((?:0|[1-9]\d*|\d*[a-zA-Z-][0-9a-zA-Z-]*)(?:\.(?:0|[1-9]\d*|\d*[a-zA-Z-][0-9a-zA-Z-]*))*))$`.
